acceptance_criteria: Zero-spend preflight confirms provider auth and cloud runner readiness before model execution.
The CodeClash run uses the frozen Vertex Gemini model, task, and $25 budget ceiling.
Raw artifacts are retained or sanitized before commit if provider logs include project identifiers.
The result records whether p1 produced a provider-backed trajectory, changed files, cost, and API-call stats.
No leaderboard, SWE-bench, production, or live-domain claim is made.

evidence.notes: Cloud CodeClash run reached Vertex predict but blocked with PERMISSION_DENIED and timed out; no provider trajectory was produced.
Ephemeral GCE runner installed Docker, CodeClash dependencies, build-essential, google-auth, and applied the frozen overlay.
Redacted CodeClash combined log records repeated aiplatform.endpoints.predict permission denial for the selected model.
Review confirms no provider capability or benchmark result is claimed and sensitive identifiers are redacted.
No production or live-domain behavior changed. The only cloud side effect was an ephemeral VM that was deleted after artifact collection.

falsifiers: The gate must be blocked if Vertex predict permission is denied for the selected model.
The gate must be blocked if p1 does not produce a provider-backed trajectory and agent stats.
The gate must be blocked if model cost or API-call stats are missing after an incomplete provider run.
The result must not claim leaderboard, SWE-bench, production, or live-domain verification.
The proof bundle must not commit token, credential JSON, account email, or Google Cloud project identifier values.
stated_goal: Run the frozen Vertex Gemini CodeClash provider-model pilot smoke under a $25 ceiling, or publish blocked/null evidence if the provider endpoint, access, cost accounting, or runner fails.
